summary: McKenzie reports that Microsoft's AdCenter advertising platform, despite its clunky interface and low volume, delivers downloads at half the cost per acquisition of Google AdWords. His simple ad for Bingo Card Creator achieved a 4% click-through rate and under 30 cents per download with zero optimization. He notes that large advertisers like Amazon bid on every keyword, but his targeted ad still performs well. He's cautiously optimistic but uncertain whether the low volume justifies further time investment in optimization.
key_points: Microsoft AdCenter delivers half the cost-per-download of Google AdWords despite a worse interface
A simple, unoptimized ad achieved 4% CTR and sub-30-cent cost per download
Low volume on AdCenter raises questions about whether optimization time is worthwhile
Large advertisers bidding on broad keyword sets don't necessarily crowd out niche software ads
money_quotes: Volume? Low. Interface? Annoying. Work flow? Terrible. Cost per download? Half of what Google is, and this required no tweaking whatsoever.
I'm buried under about four ads for places like Amazon which apparently bid on every word in the English language.
That is my target for reasonably profitability with advertising.
This might do a bit better if I did some optimization. I don't know if the time invested is worth the volume, though.
key_insight: Less popular advertising platforms can deliver dramatically lower acquisition costs precisely because fewer advertisers compete there, even if the volume and tooling are inferior.
